summary.en: The city's central hub, where modern riverfront development meets historic commercial blocks. It's a polished area with scenic walks, cultural institutions, and a mix of dining options.
A historic district with a fiercely independent and bohemian spirit, defined by restored brick buildings, a thriving farmers' market, and a strong sense of local creativity.

name.en: Olde Main & Shipyard District (Artsy & Industrial Grit)

summary.en: A former industrial corridor in the midst of a creative renaissance, this district is defined by its raw, gritty character and a burgeoning, unpolished arts scene.
A peaceful, transportive residential neighborhood that feels like a step back in time, known for its grand historic homes and shady, tree-lined streets.
A down-to-earth, residential part of the city that offers a taste of authentic, everyday Green Bay through its classic local institutions and quiet river trails.
A charming, historic river town with a highly walkable downtown, beautiful parks on the water, and a slightly more relaxed pace than Green Bay proper.

description.en: A cozy, no-fuss coffee shop that feels like the city's living room, perfect for grabbing a quality latte and a pastry.
An industrial-chic brewery that also serves coffee, transitioning from a remote-work haven to a lively evening taproom.
A sophisticated, speakeasy-style cocktail lounge known for its inventive drinks and intimate, upscale atmosphere.
Just over the bridge, this eclectic and art-filled cafe serves fantastic, inventive sandwiches, soups, and salads in a quirky, cozy interior.
A bustling restaurant on the CityDeck with a massive patio, serving classic Wisconsin fare with views of the Fox River.
An upscale choice for creative, New American cuisine with innovative tasting menus and a focus on presentation.
A multi-level urban park and boardwalk stretching along the Fox River, featuring native plantings and great views of the city's bridges.
A museum of regional history that often hosts quirky temporary displays revealing the area's hidden stories and eccentricities.
A stunningly restored 1930s Spanish Colonial Revival theater whose glowing marquee and ornate facade are a downtown landmark.
A classic independent record store where you can flip through vinyl bins to discover local Wisconsin bands.
The quintessential bohemian cafe and community hub, with walls covered in local art and a vegetarian-friendly menu.
Housed in a magnificent, restored 1899 train depot, this brewery's rooftop patio offers one of the best urban views in Green Bay.
An unassuming spot serving some of the most authentic and delicious tacos in the city, a true local favorite.
A sprawling food experience where you can graze on offerings from food trucks and sample local cheeses (Wednesdays, May-Oct).
A restored Art Deco microcinema and lounge that hosts the Green Bay Film Festival and shows independent films.
A key stop on the heritage trail featuring bronze statues of Packers legends, celebrating the team's deep roots in this neighborhood.
Wander the alleys off Broadway to find a growing collection of public art that tells the story of the area's history and creative energy.
A small, quiet courtyard tucked behind the buildings on Broadway, offering a peaceful spot to take a break from the main street bustle.
A cozy coffee and wine bar that feels like a neighborhood living room, great for seeing the local creative community.
A relaxed and unpretentious wine bar perfect for discovering new wines without any stuffiness. A true hidden gem.
On the edge of the district, this market and restaurant focuses on locally sourced ingredients and gourmet sandwiches.
Offers a curated selection of global wines and delicious small plates in a sophisticated setting.
The heart of the district's creative scene, this non-profit art center in a former warehouse features exhibits from local artists.
A unique and quirky gallery dedicated to art with an automotive theme, fitting the neighborhood's industrial roots.

name.en: Ray Nitschke Memorial Bridge (Main St Bridge)

description.en: This functional bridge offers a fantastic vantage point for one of the best views of the downtown skyline, especially at sunset.
Look up at the brick buildings along Main and Cedar streets to see faded advertisements from a century ago.
A bright, modern coffee shop on the edge of the district, known for high-quality, ethically sourced coffee.
A charming, family-run Italian restaurant located in a historic house, offering an intimate dining experience.
An upscale-casual spot known for its steak and seafood, a neighborhood favorite for a nice dinner out.
A beautiful 1837 Greek Revival house offering a glimpse into Green Bay's early history and frontier life.
Wander down S. Monroe Ave and Porlier St to see a stunning outdoor museum of architectural styles, from Queen Anne to Italianate.
A lovely, quiet park in the heart of the neighborhood, perfect for taking a break and admiring the surrounding historic homes.
As you walk the main streets, peek down the alleys to spot the original carriage houses behind the grand homes.
A true neighborhood brewery with a cozy, welcoming taproom focusing on well-crafted, traditional beer styles.
A legendary, cash-only burger joint serving the community since 1934. A no-frills, time-capsule experience.
A beloved local breakfast institution with classic American diner fare and a bustling, unpretentious atmosphere.

name.en: Fox River State Recreational Trail (East Bank)

description.en: This section of the trail offers a quieter and more natural perspective of the river, perfect for a long, contemplative walk.
A historic baseball stadium built in 1929 with a classic, old-timey feel, a great example of Depression-era architecture.
A smaller, more intimate trail that follows the East River, a hidden gem for walkers looking to escape popular routes.
A destination for coffee aficionados, they roast their own beans in-house in a minimalist, industrial space.
A family-owned institution making chocolate since 1899, with legendary hot chocolate and vast candy counters.
A historic hotel restaurant serving classic, old-school Wisconsin supper club fare that feels like a delicious step back in time.
A European-style bakery and cafe famous for its incredible pastries, cakes, and gourmet sandwiches.

name.en: De Pere Riverwalk and Voyageur Park

description.en: A beautiful trail and park system along the Fox River, highlighted by the lock and dam where you can watch boats pass through.
This historic bridge offers stunning views of the De Pere dam and the rushing Fox River, especially at sunrise or sunset.
Wander through the picturesque grounds of this liberal arts college, which features beautiful brick buildings and a quiet shrine.

prompt.en: Capture a photo where the modern architecture of the downtown core is reflected in the Fox River.
Find a spot where a historic brick building stands next to a modern glass one and photograph the contrast.
Take a selfie in a small park or public seating area, showcasing a moment of calm in the professional bustle.
Photograph one of the downtown bridges from an interesting angle along the riverwalk.
As evening approaches, capture the warm glow of a restaurant or bar's lights spilling onto the sidewalk.
Take a picture of the exterior of a theater or museum, focusing on a unique architectural detail.
Buy a coffee or a pastry from a local cafe and take an artistic photo of it with the city street in the background.
Take a photo of people enjoying a stroll along the CityDeck or riverfront.
Find and photograph an interesting or historic business sign.
Capture an image that represents the 'professional bustle' of the neighborhood during the day.
Create a close-up photo focusing on the textures of the urban environment: cobblestones, metal benches, or brickwork.
Find a piece of public sculpture or art and take a creative photo with it.
From across the river, take a selfie with the downtown skyline in the background.
Take a photo looking straight up at the tallest building you can find.
Photograph a locally brewed beer or a craft cocktail at a downtown establishment.
Capture a photo that you feel represents the 'bohemian spirit' of the Broadway District.
Photograph the architectural details of one of the restored turn-of-the-century brick buildings.
Find a colorful mural in an alley or on the side of a building and take a picture.
Take a selfie in front of an independent, locally-owned shop that catches your eye.
Find a flyer or poster for a local community event and photograph it.
Photograph a unique food item from a local eatery or the farmer's market.
Find a piece of art displayed in a cafe or shop window and take a picture of it.
Discover a quiet courtyard or alleyway and capture its peaceful atmosphere.
Find a reference to the Green Bay Packers (a statue, sign, or merchandise) and photograph it.
Capture the vibrant energy of the main street, with its mix of people, cars, and storefronts.
Take an interesting photo of a creative window display in one of the local shops.
Take a selfie enjoying a drink at a local coffeehouse, capturing the unique vibe of the place.
Find a building that has clearly been repurposed from its original use (like a depot turned brewery) and photograph it.
Photograph the interesting shadows cast by the historic buildings in the late afternoon sun.
Take a photo of a detail that you think gives this neighborhood its unique character.
Capture a photo that embodies the 'raw, gritty character' of the district.
Find a sign of the burgeoning arts scene against an industrial backdrop, like a gallery in an old warehouse.
Look up and find a faded 'ghost sign' on a brick building and photograph it.
Take a photo from one of the bridges, capturing the view of the river and the city.
Take a selfie that shows you exploring the creative, unpolished side of the city.
Get a close-up shot of the textures of an old industrial building: peeling paint, rusted metal, or weathered brick.
Find a small, unassuming spot like a cozy bar or quirky gallery and capture its entrance.
If the time is right, capture the sunset colors over the Fox River from this district.
Frame a photo of an ordinary object or scene in a way that makes it look like a piece of art.
Find and photograph a piece of old machinery, a railroad track, or another relic of the area's industrial past.
Capture the cozy, welcoming atmosphere inside a local coffee or wine bar.
Take a photo down an empty street or alleyway, emphasizing the quiet, unpolished feel of the area.
Find something made from recycled or industrial materials and take a photo of it.
Take a photo of your drink or snack from a local spot, presented as the fuel for your artistic exploration.
Capture a moment where sunlight beautifully illuminates an otherwise gritty or industrial scene.
Find the most impressive historic home on the block and take a grand 'portrait' of it.
Get a close-up shot of an interesting architectural detail: stained glass, intricate woodwork, or a unique turret.
Take a selfie on one of the shady, tree-lined streets, capturing the peaceful atmosphere.
Photograph a welcoming front porch, focusing on details like rocking chairs, hanging plants, or a decorative door.
Take a photo that makes the neighborhood look like it's from a different era. Try a black and white filter!
Find a quiet park bench and take a photo from its perspective, looking out at the surrounding homes.
Peek down an alley and photograph a historic carriage house hidden behind a grand home.
Create a photo collage of three different, beautifully painted historic homes.
Capture the long shadows cast by the mature trees across a well-manicured lawn.
Photograph the most elegant or imposing front doorway you can find.
Pick your favorite house in the neighborhood and take a photo of it, explaining in the caption why you chose it.
Find a spot where ivy, a large tree, or a beautiful garden perfectly complements a historic home and capture it.
Find a historical plaque or marker on a house or in a park and take a photo.
Take a photo down the middle of a quiet residential street (when safe!) to emphasize the peacefulness.
Focus on a single, interesting window of a historic house. What stories could it tell?
Take a photo that captures the 'down-to-earth, everyday' vibe of the East Side.
Find and photograph a classic, vintage-style sign for a local business.
Take a selfie at a local brewery, capturing the cozy, welcoming taproom atmosphere.
Capture a peaceful scene from your walk along the Fox River or East River trail.
Photograph the exterior of a beloved, long-standing local restaurant or diner.
Take a picture of a classic, no-frills meal like a burger or pancakes from a neighborhood spot.
Photograph a typical residential street, showing what everyday life looks like in this part of the city.
Find a local park, school, or community center and take a photo that shows its role in the neighborhood.
Find a small pocket of nature along one of the trails and capture it in a photo.
Capture an image that suggests the presence of 'regulars' at a local spot (without invading privacy).
Find beauty in an ordinary, unpretentious scene in the neighborhood.
Take a photo of a place that looks like a classic local hangout.
Find a building or business that looks like it hasn't changed in decades and photograph it.
Take a photo looking down a stretch of the recreational trail, showing where it leads.
Take a selfie showing you enjoying the simple, authentic pleasures of the East Side.
Capture a single photo that you think best represents the 'charming, historic river town' vibe of De Pere.
Take a photo of the water flowing over the dam on the Fox River.
Take a selfie enjoying a chocolate, pastry, or coffee from one of De Pere's famous local spots.
Photograph the historic storefronts along Broadway or Wisconsin Street.
Capture a scenic view from Voyageur Park or the Riverwalk.
Take a photo from the Claude Allouez Bridge, focusing on the view of the town or the river.
Find a peaceful spot on the St. Norbert College campus and take a photo of the beautiful brick architecture.
Find a restaurant that has a classic, old-school Wisconsin supper club feel and photograph its entrance.
If you're lucky, capture a boat passing through the lock and dam system.
Take a photo that showcases how walkable and pleasant the downtown area is.
Find a quiet bench with a view of the river and take a photo from that spot.
Photograph a building or sign that feels like a true De Pere institution.
Take a close-up, artistic shot of a handcrafted item, like a piece of chocolate, a pastry, or a perfectly poured coffee.
Find a beautiful flower box or public garden and capture its colors.
Take a photo of people enjoying the relaxed pace of the town, perhaps sitting at an outdoor cafe or strolling by the river.
